Portfolio
Incorrect password

Problem

Solution

Design Exploration

Two layout options were explored to solve a key content challenge — how to surface enough information to be meaningful without overwhelming the scroll experience. A core consideration was the mobile tray: teaser copy needed to have enough weight to make opening it feel worthwhile, feeding naturally into the full content without truncating mid-thought or leaving the user to expand for a single extra line. Option B was the preferred route — summarising all content upfront with visible CTAs that encouraged the next step, creating a more seamless and purposeful flow through the experience.

Research & Discovery

Four core customer beliefs shaped the design direction — surfaced through research and usability testing across a broad range of customer archetypes.

Finding What's Right For Me

With a myriad of products and customer archetypes, personalisation is key — customers need to feel like they belong and can find what they're after with ease. Labelling, filters, signposting, iconography and messaging all play a role in getting this right.

Transparency Is Important

As sustainability becomes a baseline expectation, customers are drawn to transparency in manufacturing and materials. Sale language, iconography, product recommendations and expected features all need to resonate clearly and honestly.

Education

Customers want to understand what they're buying. Personalisation features, fit guidance and clear product information help customers feel confident — particularly important given the breadth of the SKIMS range and the diversity of its audience.

Product Is Key

Whether exploring or searching with intent, product browsing is the core goal of any shopping experience. It must be well thought-out and intuitive — filters, signposting and interaction patterns all need to work seamlessly together.

Key Decision

Constraints & Usability Challenges

Challenges

Usability testing surfaced key gaps between user expectations and interface behaviour across product detail and landing pages.

Iconography & Labelling

"Buy more & save" was frequently perceived as a primary action rather than a promotional message, misaligning user intent with interface design.

Scroll Behaviour

Users expected the hero image to reset on scroll-up, rather than cycling through product angles — breaking their mental model of control.

Generational Differences

Interpretation of interaction patterns varied across age groups, impacting how users understood hierarchy, affordance, and intent.

Design Response

—

Refined labelling to reduce ambiguity and align with user intent.

—

Adjusted interaction patterns to better match expected behaviours.

—

Simplified visual hierarchy across product pages.

—

Designed for broader usability across varying levels of digital familiarity.

Outcome

Background

Experience

Digitas Health
Nov 2023 – Present
End-to-end UX across a portfolio of global pharmaceutical clients including Pfizer, Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk, and Takeda, often operating as the sole UX specialist across multiple accounts. I design and deliver a wide range of digital experiences — from DSPs and eDetails to websites, mobile interactions, and SEO-driven content — within complex regulatory and compliance environments. Embedded within Strategy, I partner closely with cross-functional teams spanning competitor analysis, market orientation, UX, and development, shaping solutions from initial brief through to build and optimisation.
AKQA
June 2021 – Oct 2023
Worked across retainer clients and pitches to envision, create and reskin websites and mobile applications for clients such as SKIMS, AiScout, Bp, Choose Love and Bacardi.

About me

Hi, I'm Eliza

I'm a UX designer driven by a deep curiosity in psychology — and how understanding human behaviour leads to sharper, more meaningful design. I work end-to-end to create experiences that busineses love, and humans can thrive on.

I'm excited by where the industry is heading. AI doesn't replace our thinking — it stretches it. It's a tool that pushes creativity further, opens up new ways of solving problems, and raises the bar for what we're capable of making.
